title: Squeezed-state semi-device-independent quantum randomness generation
abstract: This paper investigates semi-device-independent quantum randomness generation with a trusted binary pure-state source and an untrusted binary detector whose side information is classical. We derive a closed-form Shannon-rate expression for this setting, depending only on the trusted Gram overlap of the two source states and the observed symmetric error probability. The key point is that the full binary-qubit POVM optimisation must include the two deterministic extreme points omitted by the projective-only treatment; including them gives a substantially lower, and correct, certified rate. The closed form is an unconditional upper bound on the certified asymptotic i.i.d.\ Shannon rate, and becomes tight on a numerically verified dual-feasibility region containing all operating points used in the paper. Outside this region the same expression remains an upper bound. We then apply the result to squeezed-coherent BPSK sources, showing how squeezing changes the trade-off between state distinguishability and certified randomness in the lossless and lossy regimes. Finally, we clarify the adversary model if the adversary is allowed to hold a detector-purification register that tags the outcome.
summary: This work derives a precise, closed-form upper bound for the rate of quantum randomness generation under semi-device-independent assumptions. By correctly solving the full POVM optimization, the authors correct previous overestimations and analyze how squeezing affects the achievable randomness rate. This is crucial for designing practical, high-security quantum random number generators.
main_problem: The paper investigates the theoretical limit for generating quantum randomness in a semi-device-independent setting.
main_result: A closed-form, unconditional upper bound for the certified asymptotic Shannon rate is derived, which is shown to be tight on a numerically verified dual-feasibility region.
method: The problem is formulated as a Linear Program (LP) over binary POVMs, requiring the inclusion of deterministic extreme points for an accurate rate calculation.
model_or_system: The model uses a trusted binary pure-state source and an untrusted binary detector whose side information is classical, specifically tested with squeezed-coherent BPSK sources.
key_observables: Certified asymptotic i.i.d. Shannon rate ($H_{	ext{cert}}$), Gram overlap ($\delta$), and single-symbol error probability ($q$).
important_parameters: Squeezing parameter ($r$), photon budget ($ ar{n}$), and the error probability $q$ relative to critical thresholds ($q_b, q_z$).
main_assumptions: The adversary's side information ($\lambda$) is assumed to be classical; the rate formula is an upper bound, though tight in the verified region.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the comparison between the extended LP closed form and projective-only treatments, mapping the rate in the $(\delta, q)$ plane, and showing the trade-off between squeezing and randomness.
paper_structure: The paper proceeds by deriving the rate expression via LP optimization, establishing the necessary and sufficient conditions for the rate to be achievable (the dual-feasibility region), and finally applying the result to the specific physical case of squeezed-coherent BPSK sources.
why_interesting: It provides rigorous, quantifiable bounds on randomness extraction from quantum sources, which is a core topic in quantum information theory and quantum optics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Extended-LP closed form Hext Sh = r · h(p) (solid) and projective-only LP value h(pproj) (dotted), on the rate- positive interval q ∈(qb, qz) at δ ∈{0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9}.

FIG. 2. Hext Sh (δ, q) on the rate-positive interior q ∈(qb, qz). Curves: qb (solid black), qz (dashed black), honest BPSK q = (1 −erf√2µeff)/2 at δ = e−2µeff (salmon). Gray: outside the displayed positive-rate branch; below qb the observation is outside the feasible moment region, while above qz the de- terministic/projective zero-cost decomposition gives the rate- zero plateau.

FIG. 3. Top: h(cos2 θ) (solid) and dual witness Lδ,q(θ) (dashed) over the full angular range θ ∈[0, π] at δ = 0.6 for the honest BPSK observation, using the active branch θ0 = π −arccos √p ∈(π/2, π). Filled marker: tangent con- tact at θ0. Bottom: residual g(θ) = h(cos2 θ) −Lδ,q(θ) at δ ∈{0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.95} for the honest (nominal) BPSK obser- vation.

FIG. 4. Hext Sh along the honest BPSK locus vs squeezing r, at fixed ¯n ∈{0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 1.0}; lossless source. Each curve runs from r = 0 to rmax(¯n) = sinh−1 √¯n where N →0. The regime µeff < 0.019 in which Hext Sh is an upper bound on Hcert rather than the certified rate occurs only on the extreme high- r tails, below the BPSK-meaningful cutoff N = 0.05, and is not used for any certified operating point.

FIG. 5. Left: maximum certified rate Hext Sh max(¯n, η) := maxr Hext Sh subject to N ≥0.05 and the dual-feasibility condition m2(δ, q; 5 × 10−3) ≥0. Right: additive squeezing benefit ∆H := Hext Sh max −Hext Sh |r=0. Honest BPSK observation under Eq. (5). Dotted line: η = 0.7.
